The king should cause the Standard to be erected for the
destruction of his enemies, in such a manner that the tip of the
Banner points tn the city of the enemy, the Banner being surround-
ed by the citizens who bow their heads in homage and who invoke
it with fruits, curds, ghee, fried grains, honey and flowers held in
their hands.
[The reading in the second line, is adopted by the com-
mentator who construes it as 3r?q^f ?5^b7:. We are at a loss to find as

reading should be Otherwise we should interpret the word
as 'by the happy and content citizens.']
